title: Resume
Resume
Resume
text:
Experience

Data Scientist
OneMagnify (Previously Splash Analytics)
Remote, USA | January 2023 - Current
As part of a boutique analytics consulting firm, I manage end-to-end data science projects across the full data stack for Fortune 500 clients in the replacement parts industry. I lead engagements from client relationship management to delivering impactful data-driven solutions.

Consulting & Client Engagement: Lead multiple high-stakes engagements with Fortune 500 clients, managing relationships, gathering requirements, and presenting tailored data solutions. My role includes conducting client meetings, setting project expectations, and delivering actionable insights, ensuring satisfaction and long-term value creation.
Data Science & Analytics: Developed weekly price recommendation models for Amazon ASINs, integrating competitive analysis, seller performance, and margin targets. Support end-of-life part forecasts using ARIMA, ETS, Prophet, and XGBoost, optimizing inventory management for discontinued replacement parts.
Business Intelligence: Developed customized HTML reports that surpassed the limitations of traditional formats like PDF/Word/PowerPoint, offering advanced features and portability. Maintained 5 Tableau workbooks on the client’s Tableau Server, delivering dashboards that provided a single view of seller performance on Amazon. These insights helped manage $30M in annual sales across 6,000 SKUs. Created competitive marketplace reports using web scraping to highlight missed revenue opportunities and counterfeit activity, helping clients strategize product offerings.
Data Engineering: Designed and implemented robust ETL pipelines, ingesting data from FTP, Google Drive, Amazon S3, and various databases into our internal systems. Enhanced existing workflows to streamline the ingestion and transformation of large datasets across clients.
DevOps & Infrastructure: Standardized the use of Git and GitHub for version control and project management, ensuring the accuracy and integrity of SQL queries across client projects. Established database schemas and standards for client data, ensuring consistency in our project folder structures, both locally and in development environments, akin to AWS EC2 and S3.



Operations Leadership Development Program
Xylem, Inc
Chicago, IL & Uniontown, PA | July 2021 - December 2022
During my two rotations as a Business Data Analyst and Continuous Improvement Engineer, I led multi-departmental teams across various sites, implementing data-driven projects and operational improvements.

Excess & Obsolete Disposition Project: Applied DMAIC/DMADV methodologies to develop a process for reducing excess and obsolete materials in warehouses, projected to generate $1M–$4M in savings over 3–5 years. Created a cross-departmental E&O task force, leveraging Process Flowcharts, Value Stream Mapping, and Control Charts to improve material rework, resale, and disposal practices.
Excess & Obsolete Identification Project: Automated the quarterly E&O reporting process by building SQL queries and Power Query-enabled Excel templates, standardizing reports across three manufacturing sites. This solution reduced the reporting process time by 90%, while meeting finance audit standards.
Physical Inventory Event Overhaul: Collaborated with Finance and Materials teams to streamline the annual cycle count event for three sites, reducing event time by one day for two sites and decreasing manager preparation time. Developed process flowcharts, work instructions, and checklists to ensure smooth execution.
SQL Reporting & Automation: Created SQL-based ad-hoc reports to validate supplier quotes, synchronize lot sizes, and measure excess inventory across work cells, improving decision-making and alignment across departments.
Tableau Reporting: Designed Tableau dashboards for internal audit teams, measuring forecasting accuracy, product quality benchmarks, and KPIs for units produced. These dashboards provided real-time insights, aiding strategic planning for Quality and Finance teams.
Continuous Improvement Workshops: Led and facilitated various workshops focused on Lean methodologies, 5S, FMEA, and Process Capability Analysis, driving continuous improvement initiatives and fostering a culture of operational excellence across multiple sites.



Undergraduate Teaching Assistant
West Virginia University
Morgantown, WV | August 2019 - May 2021
As a Teaching Assistant in the Industrial Engineering department, I supported student learning across four key courses, contributing to curriculum development and enhancing student comprehension through hands-on teaching and guidance.

Spring 2021 | IENG 220: Re-engineering Management Systems
Fall 2020 | IENG 305: Intro to Systems Engineering
Spring 2020 | IENG 331: Computer Applications in Industrial Engineering
Fall 2019 | IENG 445: Project Management for Engineers



Manufacturing Engineer Intern
JLG Industries
McConnellsburg, PA | June 2019 - August 2019
During my internship at JLG Industries, I contributed to process improvements and operational efficiency on the manufacturing floor, working closely with engineering teams on key projects.

Test - Inspect - Green Tag (TIG) Line: Conducted a comprehensive 6S audit to optimize workstation layouts by eliminating unnecessary equipment and parts, which improved workflow and increased efficiency. Developed Standard Work Instructions (SWIs) from scratch for five stations, covering three different products, ensuring consistency and reliability in the inspection and tagging processes.
Tire Manipulator: Implemented a scanner-based system for defect entry into the Electronic Quality Control (EQC) system, standardizing the defect entry process and improving data accuracy. Designed detailed floor plans for both current and future station layouts using laser measurements and AutoCAD, enabling optimized workspace planning for the Tire Manipulator project.

title: Resume
text:
Education

Bachelor of Science in Industrial Engineering
West Virginia University
Morgantown, WV
Industrial engineering is the discipline of engineering concerned with the design, improvement, and installation of integrated systems of people, material, information, equipment, and energy to assure performance, reliability, maintainability, schedule adherence, and cost control.


Certifications

Lean Six Sigma Green Belt | Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
Continuous Improvement Fundamentals | Oshkosh Corporation
Eligible for Certified Associate in Project Management (CAPM) | Project Management Institute

title: Ozan Ozbeker
text:
Hello! I’m Ozan, an industrial engineer turned data scientist. My passion for building and problem-solving started early, from playing with Legos as a kid to crafting worlds in Minecraft. That same drive led me to industrial engineering and, eventually, to the world of data science, where I combine creativity with technology to tackle complex challenges.
I’m currently based in Morgantown, WV & working remotely at OneMagnify.
When I’m not immersed in data or coding, you’ll likely find me at a live music event, especially if it’s Metal(core), pop-punk, or EDM. If I’m not at a concert, I’m probably cycling, playing the guitar, or experimenting with a new data science project.
On this site, you’ll find my resume, projects, and blog, where I explore tools and technologies in the data world alongside other topics that spark my curiosity.
Feel free to reach out and connect—I’d love to hear from you!
